title: silene venosa subsp. vulgaris
intro: Silene venosa subsp. vulgaris, also known as Wood Sorrel or Red Wood Sorrel, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its reddish-purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. It is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestive issues, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral properties. The leaves and stems of Silene venosa subsp. vulgaris can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and are also used in salads and as a garnish for various dishes.
